title: Expense Reimbursement Policy
content: Business expenses must be submitted within 30 days of the transaction. Original receipts are required for all expenses over $25.

title: Corporate Credit Card Usage
content: Corporate cards are for business use only. Personal purchases are strictly prohibited and may result in card cancellation and disciplinary action.

title: Procurement Thresholds
content: Purchases over $5000 require three competitive bids. Purchases over $50,000 require CFO approval and a formal contract.

title: Travel and Subsistence Rates
content: Daily per diem rates for international travel are based on GSA standards. Hotel bookings must not exceed $250 per night in major cities.

title: Accounts Payable Process
content: Standard payment terms for vendors are Net 30. Urgent payments require a written justification from a VP or above.

title: Budget Allocation and Monitoring
content: Department budgets are reviewed quarterly. Overages exceeding 10% must be reported to the Finance Planning and Analysis (FP&A) team.

title: Audit and Compliance Framework
content: The company undergoes an annual external financial audit. All departments must cooperate with internal audit requests within 48 hours.

title: Tax Compliance and Reporting
content: The company adheres to all local and international tax laws. Transfer pricing documentation is maintained for all cross-border transactions.

title: Petty Cash Management
content: Petty cash is limited to $200 per office. Reconciliation must be performed weekly by the office manager and verified by Finance.

title: Inventory Control Policy
content: Physical inventory counts are conducted bi-annually. Discrepancies over 5% of total value require a formal investigation.

title: Charitable Giving and Sponsorships
content: All charitable donations must be aligned with company values and approved by the Social Responsibility Committee and Finance.

title: Anti-Money Laundering (AML)
content: The company performs 'Know Your Customer' (KYC) checks for all enterprise clients to prevent financial crimes.

title: Payroll Processing Standards
content: Payroll is processed on the 25th of each month. Any changes to bank details must be submitted through the HR portal by the 15th.

title: Fixed Asset Management
content: Assets valued over $1000 are capitalized and tagged. Disposal of fixed assets requires a signed Asset Retirement Form.

title: Revenue Recognition Policy
content: Revenue is recognized when the performance obligation is satisfied, in accordance with ASC 606 and IFRS 15 standards.

title: Cash Flow Management
content: Weekly cash flow forecasts are prepared by the Treasury team. Minimum liquidity levels must be maintained at all times.

title: Signature Authority Limits
content: The CEO has unlimited signature authority. VPs are limited to $250,000. Directors are limited to $50,000 for operational contracts.

title: Currency Exchange Risk Management
content: The company uses forward contracts to hedge against significant currency fluctuations in major operating markets.

title: Vendor Setup and Maintenance
content: New vendors must provide a W-9 form and banking verification. Changes to vendor bank details require a verification call to a known contact.

title: Internal Controls (Sarbanes-Oxley)
content: The company maintains internal controls over financial reporting to ensure accuracy and prevent fraud, aligned with SOX 404 standards.

title: Dividend and Capital Return Policy
content: Decisions regarding dividends or share buybacks are made by the Board of Directors based on financial performance and capital needs.

title: Intercompany Transaction Pricing
content: Transactions between subsidiaries are priced at arm's length to comply with international tax and accounting standards.

title: Financial Record Retention
content: Financial records, including invoices and tax filings, must be retained for 10 years in a secure and accessible format.

title: Gift and Hospitality Reporting
content: Any hospitality received from vendors valued over $100 must be disclosed to the Finance and Compliance departments.

title: Project Cost Accounting
content: Costs for internal R&D projects are tracked separately. Capitalization of internal-use software costs follows FASB ASC 350-40.
